rationale: The score is identical (6/10) in both evaluations, and the justifications, strengths, and concerns are substantively the same — strong software background, some non-recent C++ experience, SQL/database knowledge, and the same three concerns (limited recent C++, no capital markets/fixed income, no Windows). The only differences are cosmetic rewordings ('various programming languages' vs 'Linux expertise', 'No mention of' vs 'Lack of') that fall within ordinary run-to-run noise. Nothing in the variant keys off the employer prestige change (re:cinq/Optilyz/GISCollective → Google/Meta/Amazon); no company name, prestige, or scale credit appears anywhere.
